hero.title: Build Your Own Self-Driving Car
hero.subtitle: Donkeycar is an open-source DIY self-driving platform for small scale cars. Built with Python and powered by machine learning, it lets you train your own autopilot using your own track and data.

features.title: Why Donkeycar?
features.subtitle: Everything you need to build, train, and race your own autonomous vehicle.

autonomousDriving.title: Autonomous Driving
autonomousDriving.description: Train your car to drive itself using behavioral cloning and end-to-end deep learning. Collect data, train a model, and watch your car navigate the track autonomously.

machineLearning.title: Machine Learning
machineLearning.description: Leverage TensorFlow and PyTorch to build neural networks that learn from your driving data. Experiment with different architectures and training strategies.

hardwareFlexibility.title: Hardware Flexibility
hardwareFlexibility.description: Run on Raspberry Pi, Jetson Nano, or any Linux computer. Support for a wide range of RC car platforms, cameras, and sensors gives you complete freedom.

openSourceCommunity.title: Open Source Community
openSourceCommunity.description: Join thousands of builders worldwide. Contribute to the codebase, share your builds, participate in virtual and real-world races, and learn together.

gettingStarted.title: Get Up and Running
gettingStarted.subtitle: From zero to autonomous driving in four simple steps.

install.title: Install Donkeycar
install.description: Set up your development environment and install the Donkeycar Python package with a single command.

build.title: Build Your Car
build.description: Assemble your RC car with a Raspberry Pi or Jetson Nano, camera module, and motor controller.

train.title: Train Your Model
train.description: Drive your car manually to collect training data, then train a neural network to learn your driving behavior.

drive.title: Drive Autonomously
drive.description: Load your trained model and let your car drive itself around the track. Iterate and improve!

community.title: Join the Community
community.subtitle: Be part of a global network of makers, engineers, and AI enthusiasts building the future of autonomous vehicles.

footer.description: An open-source DIY self-driving platform for small scale cars. Learn AI by building and racing autonomous vehicles.
footer.copyright: Donkeycar Project. Open source under MIT License.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Everything you need to know about building, training, and racing with Donkeycar.

vehicle.title: Vehicle API
vehicle.description: Core vehicle class for managing parts, driving loops, and data recording.

parts.title: Parts API
parts.description: Modular components for cameras, actuators, controllers, and AI pilots.

training.title: Training API
training.description: Tools for data augmentation, model training, and transfer learning.

firstCar.title: Build Your First Car
firstCar.description: Step-by-step guide to assembling hardware and installing software.

firstModel.title: Train Your First Model
firstModel.description: Collect data, train a neural network, and test autonomous driving.

simulator.title: Using the Simulator
simulator.description: Get started with the Donkeycar simulator for virtual training and testing.

hardware.title: What hardware do I need?
hardware.description: A compatible RC car, Raspberry Pi 4 or Jetson Nano, wide-angle camera, and a portable battery pack.

cost.title: How much does it cost?
cost.description: A basic build costs around $200-$300 USD depending on components and your region.

experience.title: Do I need ML experience?
experience.description: No! Donkeycar is designed for beginners. Basic Python knowledge is helpful but not required.

hero.title: Powerful Features
hero.subtitle: Donkeycar provides a complete toolkit for building autonomous vehicles — from data collection to model deployment.

behavioral.title: Behavioral Cloning
behavioral.description: Record your driving and train a neural network to mimic your behavior on the track.

endToEnd.title: End-to-End Learning
endToEnd.description: Raw camera images go in, steering and throttle commands come out. No hand-coded rules.

realtime.title: Real-Time Inference
realtime.description: Run trained models at 20+ FPS on edge devices for responsive autonomous control.

tensorflow.title: TensorFlow & Keras
tensorflow.description: Build and train models using the industry-standard deep learning framework.

pytorch.title: PyTorch Support
pytorch.description: Use PyTorch for flexible model development and research experimentation.

transfer.title: Transfer Learning
transfer.description: Fine-tune pre-trained models to achieve better performance with less data.

raspberryPi.title: Raspberry Pi
raspberryPi.description: Full support for Raspberry Pi 4 and 5 with optimized camera and GPIO integration.

jetson.title: NVIDIA Jetson
jetson.description: GPU-accelerated inference on Jetson Nano and Orin for maximum performance.

sensors.title: Sensor Suite
sensors.description: Support for cameras, LIDAR, IMU, encoders, and ultrasonic distance sensors.

hero.title: Getting Started
hero.subtitle: Follow these guides to go from unboxing to autonomous driving.

installation.title: Installation
installation.description: Install Donkeycar on your computer and your car's single-board computer.

hardware.title: Hardware Setup
hardware.description: Assemble your autonomous vehicle with these components.
items: Raspberry Pi 4 (4GB+) or NVIDIA Jetson Nano

software.title: Software Configuration
software.description: Configure your car's software for data collection and autonomous driving.
steps: Edit myconfig.py to set your camera and steering parameters
Test manual driving with a web controller or gamepad
Verify data recording to the tub directory

firstModel.title: Your First Model
firstModel.description: Collect data and train your first self-driving model.
steps: Drive 10-20 laps around your track collecting data
Transfer tub data to your training computer
Copy the model back to your car and test autonomous mode

hero.title: Community
hero.subtitle: Donkeycar is built by a passionate global community of makers, racers, and AI enthusiasts.

github.title: GitHub
github.description: Browse the source code, report issues, and submit pull requests. The Donkeycar repository is the heart of the project.

discord.title: Discord
discord.description: Join our active Discord server to chat with other builders, get help with your build, and share your progress.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.description: Whether you write code, improve documentation, or help others in the community — every contribution matters.

events.title: Races & Events
events.description: Participate in virtual and real-world Donkeycar races held around the globe. Test your skills against other builders.
